title: leucanthemum yezoense
intro: Leucanthemum yezoense, also known as the Japanese oxeye daisy, is a type of flowering plant native to Japan. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are thought to be attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The health benefits of Leucanthemum yezoense are said to include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and exhibit antioxidant activity, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, or extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress.
